findings.title: HQ-VSR video processing pipeline (Sec 3.3, Fig 3, Tab 1c/1d) not in repo
findings.claim: The four-step pipeline (metadata/scene/quality filtering + optical-flow motion-area detection and cropping, Eq. 8) that builds the 2,055-video HQ-VSR dataset is not present anywhere in the repo; grep for motion_mask/bounding_box/aesthetic/scenedetect/optical-flow-score returns nothing, and the README TODO marks it unreleased.
findings.concern: HQ-VSR construction is the paper's second headline contribution and underpins Tab. 1c (dataset ablation) and Tab. 1d (+Filter/+Motion ablation); without the pipeline these ablation results cannot be reproduced and the dataset cannot be regenerated.
findings.resolution: Release the pipeline scripts (metadata/scene/quality filtering, motion-area detection/cropping) or the exact configs and thresholds (Aesthetic>6.5, CLIP-IQA>0.4, FasterVQA>0.6, DOVER>0.7, τ, padding p) used to produce HQ-VSR.

findings.title: No FasterVQA computation script for Tab. 2 / Fig. 1 FasterVQA column
findings.quote: > **TODO:** Add metric computation scripts for FasterVQA, DOVER, and $E^*_{warp}$.
findings.claim: FasterVQA is reported in Tab. 2 and Fig. 1, but no script computes it: a repo-wide grep for 'fastervqa'/'faster_vqa' over all .py files returns no hits (see _audit_code/out/missing_eval_deps.json: fastervqa_script_present=false).
findings.concern: A reported metric column has no traceable computation in the repo, so those FasterVQA numbers cannot be reproduced from the released code; the authors acknowledge this in the README TODO.
findings.resolution: Add the FasterVQA evaluation script (or document the exact external repo/commit and command used).

findings.title: eval_dover.py imports a DOVER package that does not exist in the repo
findings.quote: from DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos import evaluate_set as dover
findings.claim: After os.chdir(script_directory) (the script's own dir, finetune/scripts/), the script imports DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos, but no 'DOVER' package/directory exists anywhere in the repo (see _audit_code/out/missing_eval_deps.json: DOVER_pkg_anywhere=false).
findings.concern: The DOVER metric reported throughout Tab. 2/Tab. 3/Fig. 1 cannot be computed with the shipped code as-is; the script raises ModuleNotFoundError on import.
findings.resolution: Vendor the DOVER package, add it as a pinned dependency with the expected import path, or document the external DOVER repo/commit and how to place it on sys.path.

findings.title: eval_ewarp.py chdir's to non-existent scripts/RAFT and imports absent ewarp
findings.quote:
raft_dir = os.path.join(original_dir, "RAFT")
os.chdir(raft_dir)
from ewarp import Ewarp as Ewarp
findings.claim: original_dir is finetune/scripts/ (set by os.chdir(script_directory) at the top), so raft_dir resolves to finetune/scripts/RAFT, which does not exist (RAFT lives at finetune/utils/RAFT); furthermore no 'ewarp.py' exists anywhere in the repo (see _audit_code/out/missing_eval_deps.json: RAFT_under_scripts=false, RAFT_under_utils=true, ewarp_module_anywhere=false).
findings.concern: The E*_warp temporal-consistency metric reported in Tab. 2 cannot be computed with the shipped code; the chdir raises FileNotFoundError and the import would raise ModuleNotFoundError.
findings.resolution: Point raft_dir at finetune/utils/RAFT (or the correct location) and add the missing ewarp.py implementation, or document the external source for both.

findings.title: inference.sh evaluates every dataset against UDM10 ground truth
findings.claim: All six eval_metrics.py calls pass --gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT while --pred varies (SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, MVSR4x, VideoLQ); 5/6 calls pair non-UDM10 predictions with UDM10 GT (see _audit_code/out/inference_sh_gt.json).
findings.concern: eval_metrics.py skips any prediction whose basename has no matching GT filename (process(): 'if has_gt and name_hr not in gt_files: continue'), so running inference.sh as written would compute full-reference metrics for the wrong/empty GT set on 5 of 6 datasets, not reproducing the Tab. 2 fidelity columns.
findings.resolution: Set --gt to the matching dataset's GT folder in each block (e.g. datasets/test/SPMCS/GT).

findings.title: eval_dover.py maps DOVER scores to names with an off-by-one (i-1)
findings.quote:
for i, name in enumerate(pred_names):
    results[name] = dover_results[i-1]
findings.claim: The loop assigns dover_results[i-1] to pred_names[i]; for i=0 this is dover_results[-1] (the last score), shifting every per-sample DOVER score by one name (see _audit_code/out/dover_offbyone.json: 4/4 mismatched, first_name_gets_last_score=true).
findings.concern: Per-sample DOVER values written to metrics_dover.json are attributed to the wrong videos; the overall average (np.mean over results.values(), L164) is unaffected since it is the same multiset, so reported Tab. 2 DOVER averages are not corrupted by this alone.
findings.resolution: Index with dover_results[i] instead of dover_results[i-1]; confirm len(dover_results)==len(pred_names) and the ordering returned by evaluate_set matches sorted(pred_names).

findings.title: SR-output padding removed as pad*4 regardless of --upscale (breaks upscale=1)
findings.claim: pad_h/pad_w are LR-pixel pads making H/W multiples of 16 (lines 228-229); the LR is then upscaled by args.upscale (line 672), so the SR padding region is pad*args.upscale, but removal hardcodes pad*4. inference.sh runs RealVSR and MVSR4x with --upscale 1 (lines 45, 58), where for non-multiple-of-16 input dims the code over-crops by 3*pad pixels (see _audit_code/out/pad_upscale_mismatch.json).
findings.concern: For RealVSR/MVSR4x (upscale=1) with input width/height not divisible by 16, the SR output is over-cropped on the right/bottom edge, slightly shifting the evaluated region and the Tab. 2 fidelity metrics for those two real-world datasets.
findings.resolution: Use pad_h*args.upscale and pad_w*args.upscale in remove_padding_and_extra_frames; confirm whether RealVSR/MVSR4x inputs are multiples of 16 (if always, impact is nil).

findings.title: Paper says DIV2K = 900 images; README/standard DIV2K_train_HR = 800
findings.quote: The image dataset is DIV2K [3], with 900 images, which follows the RealESRGAN [38] degradation process.
findings.claim: The paper (Sec 4.1) states 900 DIV2K images; the repo README lists DIV2K-HR with 800 images and links DIV2K_train_HR.zip (the standard DIV2K training split is exactly 800; 900 = 800 train + 100 val).
findings.concern: Minor inconsistency in the reported image-dataset size between paper and released code; does not affect headline conclusions but the exact training-set composition for Stage-2 is ambiguous.
findings.resolution: Clarify whether 800 or 900 DIV2K images were used for Stage-2 (and whether the 100 DIV2K validation images were included).
